mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in retail sales signals rising household demand and near-term revenue momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the sales trend is above its own 6-month average.
failure_condition: Reject if positive retail-sales trend states deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of triggered observations over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment exceeding 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, reducing expected corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25% and CPI inflation is not accelerating, monetary accommodation lowers discount rates without an inflation shock, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change <= CPIAUCSL 12-month change
failure_condition: Reject if zero-rate non-accelerating-inflation states have a 60-day forward return mean that is not above the unconditional mean by at least 25 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production larger than 0.5% signals falling output and weaker operating leverage, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if industrial-production contraction triggers have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations or Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing above their 6-month moving average signals improving construction demand and credit transmission, leading cyclical earnings upward and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST upside crossings fail to produce above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of walk-forward triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains combined with a falling unemployment rate confirm broad labor-market repair, raising household income expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE falls over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if joint payroll-growth and unemployment-decline states do not outperform other labor states by at least 50 basis points over 20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: CPI acceleration while the federal funds rate remains pinned below 0.25% signals a negative real-rate inflation scare, pressuring valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds CPIAUCSL 12-month change and FEDFUNDS < 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered CPI acceleration at the zero lower bound is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth over the latest available quarter indicates expanding aggregate demand and earnings capacity, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive real-GDP growth crossings have a 60-day forward S&P 500 return hit rate below 50% across two consecutive rolling 3-year windows.
